activities.text:
**Overview of Philips Spectral CT 7500**

**General Description**
- The Philips Spectral CT 7500 is a computed tomography (CT) X-ray system designed to produce cross-sectional images of the body using computer reconstruction of X-ray transmission data from various angles and planes.
- It utilizes a dual-layer detector to acquire both high- and low-energy X-ray spectra simultaneously, enabling advanced material analysis and the generation of images at selectable energy levels.
- The system provides information about the chemical composition of body materials and contrast agents, supporting quantification and graphical display of attenuation, material density, and effective atomic number for diagnostic purposes [1].

**Key Features**
- **Always-on Spectral Imaging:** Detector-based spectral technology is always active, requiring no special protocols or separate reconstructions. This allows for both conventional and spectral results from a single scan, streamlining workflow and reducing the need for multiple or additional scans [1]​[2].
- **Fast Scanning:** Features a fast 0.27-second rotation time and a large 80 cm bore for increased patient comfort. The high-performance table supports speeds up to 60 cm/s and a maximum patient weight of 307 kg [1]​[2].
- **Advanced Clinical Applications:** Supports a wide range of applications across radiology, cardiology, oncology, neurology, and emergency/trauma care. Enables comprehensive cardiac evaluations, enhanced lesion detectability, tumor assessment, and fast neuro assessments [1]​[2].
- **Spectral Results:** Offers a broad range of spectral imaging results, including MonoE (40–200 keV), virtual non-contrast, iodine density, Z-effective, uric acid, calcium-suppressed, and electron density maps. These results aid in tissue characterization, improved visualization of iodine-enhanced tissues, and artifact reduction [1].
- **Workflow and Automation:** The iPatient user interface and exam cards allow for streamlined, patient-centric imaging, reducing decision points and clicks, and improving scan-to-scan consistency. Spectral results can be pre-set, modified during planning, or created post-scan [1].
- **Image Quality and Reconstruction:** Utilizes advanced reconstruction algorithms such as IMR and iDose 4 for improved image quality at lower doses. The system supports high spatial and low-contrast resolution, with most reference protocols reconstructed in under one minute [1].
- **Patient Throughput:** Designed for high patient throughput, capable of handling up to 270 patients per day, with potential for more than 400 patients/day in 24-hour operation [1].
- **Energy and Environmental Considerations:** Ready-to-scan energy usage is 21 kW, with up to 149 kW during scan mode. The system is RoHS 2 and REACH compliant, with circularity programs and service/spare parts available [1].

**Clinical Benefits**
- Enables fast, confident diagnoses, potentially reducing the need for follow-up or additional scans.
- Supports advanced cardiac imaging, including coronary assessment, perfusion, and delayed enhancement studies, all without increased radiation dose.
- Provides up to 96% certainty in differentiating cysts from lesions and up to 97% diagnostic sensitivity compared to conventional CT [2].

**Technical Specifications (Selected)**
- **Generator Power:** 120 kW
- **Slices:** 512
- **Coverage:** 80 mm x 2 (double coverage)
- **Bore Size:** 800 mm
- **Rotation Speed:** 0.27s
- **Spectral MonoE Range:** 40–200 keV
- **Image Matrix:** 512 x 512 (with options for higher matrices in conventional mode)
- **Local Storage:** 7.6 TB hard drive for spectral results [1].

**Summary**
The Philips Spectral CT 7500 is a high-performance, always-on spectral CT system designed to deliver advanced diagnostic capabilities, fast workflow, and broad clinical versatility, supporting confident decision-making and efficient patient care across multiple specialties [1]​[2]​[1]​[2].

[1]: https://share.philips.com/sites/AIAssistant/Shared%20Documents/CT/Spectral%20CT/Philips%20Spectral%20CT%207500%20-%20Premium%20Specifications%20-%20SW%205.2%202024.pdf "Philips Spectral CT 7500 - Premium Specifications - SW 5.2 2024.pdf"

The Spectral CT 7500 is a computed tomography X-ray system intended to produce cross-sectional images of the body by computer reconstruction  of X-ray transmission data taken at different angles and planes. This device may include signal analysis and display equipment, patient and equipment  supports, component parts, and accessories. The Spectral CT 7500 system acquires one CT data set – composed of data from a higher-energy detected X-ray spectrum and a lower-energy detected X-ray spectrum. The two spectrums may be used to analyze the differences in the energy dependence of the attenuation coefficient of different materials. This allows for the generation of images at energies selected from the available spectrum and to provide information about the chemical composition of the body materials and/or contrast agents. Additionally, materials analysis provides for the quantification and graphical display of attenuation, material density, and effective atomic number.  This information may be used by a trained healthcare professional as a diagnostic tool for the visualization and analysis of anatomical and  pathological structures. The images and descriptions contained herein provide technical specifications and optional features which may not be included with the standard system configuration. Contact your local Philips Representative for complete specific system details. Some or all of the products, features, and accessories shown or described herein may not be available in your market. Please contact your local Philips Representative for availability. The Spectral CT 7500 is a commercial configuration of the Spectral CT device.
</page_14>

Help the customer service agent by writing a response that includes the following:

#Contextualized_Question - Preserving the original language, rewrite the follow up user question or command on the #User_question so that the conversation history (if any) is taken into consideration. #Bing_Query -  Containing only main keywords, preserving the original language, sufficient to retrieve documents related to the Contextualized question. The list of keywords should have no special characters and be space separated.


#Snippet - Generating a hypothetical document that can answer the Contextualized question. Keep the hypothetical document to less than 10 sentences.

#1. #Contextualized_Question
#2. #Bing_Query
#3. #Snippet

- **Fast Scanning:** Features a fast 0.27-second rotation time and a large 80 cm bore for increased patient comfort. The high-performance table supports speeds up to 60 cm/s and a maximum patient weight of 307 kg [1]​[2].
- **Advanced Clinical Applications:** Supports a wide range of applications across radiology, cardiology, oncology, neurology, and emergency/trauma care. Enables comprehensive cardiac evaluations, enhanced lesion detectability, tumor assessment, and fast neuro assessments [1]​[2].
- **Spectral Results:** Offers a broad range of spectral imaging results, including MonoE (40–200 keV), virtual non-contrast, iodine density, Z-effective, uric acid, calcium-suppressed, and electron density maps. These results aid in tissue characterization, improved visualization of iodine-enhanced tissues, and artifact reduction [1].
- **Workflow and Automation:** The iPatient user interface and exam cards allow for streamlined, patient-centric imaging, reducing decision points and clicks, and improving scan-to-scan consistency. Spectral results can be pre-set, modified during planning, or created post-scan [1].
- **Image Quality and Reconstruction:** Utilizes advanced reconstruction algorithms such as IMR and iDose 4 for improved image quality at lower doses. The system supports high spatial and low-contrast resolution, with most reference protocols reconstructed in under one minute [1].
- **Patient Throughput:** Designed for high patient throughput, capable of handling up to 270 patients per day, with potential for more than 400 patients/day in 24-hour operation [1].
- **Energy and Environmental Considerations:** Ready-to-scan energy usage is 21 kW, with up to 149 kW during scan mode. The system is RoHS 2 and REACH compliant, with circularity programs and service/spare parts available [1].

**Clinical Benefits**
- Enables fast, confident diagnoses, potentially reducing the need for follow-up or additional scans.
- Supports advanced cardiac imaging, including coronary assessment, perfusion, and delayed enhancement studies, all without increased radiation dose.
- Provides up to 96% certainty in differentiating cysts from lesions and up to 97% diagnostic sensitivity compared to conventional CT [2].

- **Slices:** 512
- **Coverage:** 80 mm x 2 (double coverage)

- **Rotation Speed:** 0.27s
- **Spectral MonoE Range:** 40–200 keV
- **Image Matrix:** 512 x 512 (with options for higher matrices in conventional mode)
- **Local Storage:** 7.6 TB hard drive for spectral results [1].

**Summary**
The Philips Spectral CT 7500 is a high-performance, always-on spectral CT system designed to deliver advanced diagnostic capabilities, fast workflow, and broad clinical versatility, supporting confident decision-making and efficient patient care across multiple specialties [1]​[2]​[1]​[2].

The Spectral CT 7500 is a computed tomography X-ray system intended to produce cross-sectional images of the body by computer reconstruction  of X-ray transmission data taken at different angles and planes. This device may include signal analysis and display equipment, patient and equipment  supports, component parts, and accessories. The Spectral CT 7500 system acquires one CT data set – composed of data from a higher-energy detected X-ray spectrum and a lower-energy detected X-ray spectrum. The two spectrums may be used to analyze the differences in the energy dependence of the attenuation coefficient of different materials. This allows for the generation of images at energies selected from the available spectrum and to provide information about the chemical composition of the body materials and/or contrast agents. The Spectral CT 7500 is a computed tomography X-ray system intended to produce cross-sectional images of the body by computer reconstruction  of X-ray transmission data taken at different angles and planes. This device may include signal analysis and display equipment, patient and equipment  supports, component parts, and accessories. The Spectral CT 7500 system acquires one CT data set – composed of data from a higher-energy detected X-ray spectrum and a lower-energy detected X-ray spectrum. The two spectrums may be used to analyze the differences in the energy dependence of the attenuation coefficient of different materials. This allows for the generation of images at energies selected from the available spectrum and to provide information about the chemical composition of the body materials and/or contrast agents. Additionally, materials analysis provides for the quantification and graphical display of attenuation, material density, and effective atomic number.  This information may be used by a trained healthcare professional as a diagnostic tool for the visualization and analysis of anatomical and  pathological structures. The images and descriptions contained herein provide technical specifications and optional features which may not be included with the standard system configuration. The Spectral CT 7500 is a computed tomography X-ray system intended to produce cross-sectional images of the body by computer reconstruction  of X-ray transmission data taken at different angles and planes. This device may include signal analysis and display equipment, patient and equipment  supports, component parts, and accessories. The Spectral CT 7500 system acquires one CT data set – composed of data from a higher-energy detected X-ray spectrum and a lower-energy detected X-ray spectrum. The two spectrums may be used to analyze the differences in the energy dependence of the attenuation coefficient of different materials. This allows for the generation of images at energies selected from the available spectrum and to provide information about the chemical composition of the body materials and/or contrast agents. Additionally, materials analysis provides for the quantification and graphical display of attenuation, material density, and effective atomic number.  This information may be used by a trained healthcare professional as a diagnostic tool for the visualization and analysis of anatomical and  pathological structures. The images and descriptions contained herein provide technical specifications and optional features which may not be included with the standard system configuration. Contact your local Philips Representative for complete specific system details. Some or all of the products, features, and accessories shown or described herein may not be available in your market. Please contact your local Philips Representative for availability. The Spectral CT 7500 is a commercial configuration of the Spectral CT device.

**CT Spectral Tumor Tracking **Assists in viewing and evaluating CT images acquired on the Philips Spectral CT 7500 scanners family for the inspection of tumors, on contrast-enhanced, soft tissue-oriented, and whole body scans. Supports lesion viewing and analysis based on different spectral data types such as iodine density maps or virtual non-contrast-enhanced images. Tissue segmentation and editing tools allow user-defined ROI, and the application provides information on physical (length, width, volume) and composition properties (effective atomic number, attenuation, and HU) of the tumor. The application supports longitudinal viewing of cases taken from different examination times.**CT Spectral Comprehensive Cardiac Analysis (part of CT CCA)** Supports automatic segmentation of cardiac chamber and coronary tree using monoE images at various energy levels, and provides information on physical (length, width, volume) and composition properties (effective atomic number, attenuation, HU) of the coronary vessel and findings along it. CT CCA promotes ease of use with enhanced automatic extraction and visualization of the coronary tree with automatic labeling of the different coronary segments. It also provides consolidated findings management module and CAD-RAS reporting embedded in the workflow to support determination of disease severity and follow-up recommendations according to the guidelines. Curved and straightened MPR images of all selected coronaries can be sent to the PACS with a single click. CT spectral CCA enhances the visual assessment of coronary vessel patency. The application also supports beam-hardening artifact reduction for the visualization of perfusion deficits and calcified plaque. **CT Spectral Advanced Vessel Analysis (AVA)** Supports in viewing and evaluating CT Angiography (CTA) cases on contrastenhanced and whole body CTA scans for the inspection of contrast-enhanced vessels. The CT Spectral AVA application provides: •	Automatic, semi-automatic and manual bone removal functions for body  and skull •	Automatic extraction of the vessel navigation path •	Lumen and vessel contours •	Automatic labeling of major vessels The advantage of using spectral from Spectral CT 7500 is in the ability to review and compare the visualization of different energy levels.**CT Spectral Viewer** CT Spectral Viewer enables spectral quantification through proprietary spectral tools, including the exclusive Spectral Magic Glass. ** Spectral Cardiac Pro** The Spectral Cardiac Pro package includes Precise Cardiac, Dynamic Myocardial Perfusion and Electron Density. Dynamic Myocardial Perfusion enables a special acquisition mode. **Spectral Precise Cardiac **Spectral Precise Cardiac is a single series of motion corrected cardiac images made by estimating and compensating of coronary artery motion in a pre-defined range around a reference phase by using overlapped short-scan data volumes. With Precise cardiac, the artifact reduction results in an 11x gain in effective temporal resolution for Spectral CT 7500, which would result in an effective temporal resolution of 12.3 ms. Precise Cardiac can be applied either in cardiac ECG-gated helical or ECG-gated axial scans (i.e., Step & Shoot Cardiac).** Spectral Neuro Essentials** Includes Jog Scan, Advanced Brain Perfusion and Spectral  Electron Density. Jog Scan provides up to 160 mm of organ coverage for perfusion studies. An axial scan is taken in one location, the couch translates to another location within a few seconds, and another axial scan is taken. These multiple data sets are registered automatically to provide extended coverage. Advanced Brain Perfusion application on console enables reviewing the perfusion image. Spectral Electron Density and Electron Density values of the tissues in the image are relative to the electron density of water. Electron Density accurately measures electron density of tissues/voxel within the image.**Spectral Radiation Oncology Plus **(option) Spectral Radiation Oncology Plus is designed for radiation therapy planning, considering the aspects of Radiation Treatment systems and simulation environment. Spectral Radiation Oncology Plus include:** •	Patient couch-flat indexed therapy table top** combination complies with AAPM TG-66 guidelines for positional accuracy** •	Spectral Electron Density **– The accurate electron density measurement allows calculation of accurate proton stopping power. dialogState.Var1:
**Overview of Philips Spectral CT 7500**

**General Description**
- The Philips Spectral CT 7500 is a computed tomography (CT) X-ray system designed to produce cross-sectional images of the body using computer reconstruction of X-ray transmission data from various angles and planes.
- It utilizes a dual-layer detector to acquire both high- and low-energy X-ray spectra simultaneously, enabling advanced material analysis and the generation of images at selectable energy levels.
- The system provides information about the chemical composition of body materials and contrast agents, supporting quantification and graphical display of attenuation, material density, and effective atomic number for diagnostic purposes [1].

**Key Features**
- **Always-on Spectral Imaging:** Detector-based spectral technology is always active, requiring no special protocols or separate reconstructions. This allows for both conventional and spectral results from a single scan, streamlining workflow and reducing the need for multiple or additional scans [1]​[2].
- **Fast Scanning:** Features a fast 0.27-second rotation time and a large 80 cm bore for increased patient comfort. The high-performance table supports speeds up to 60 cm/s and a maximum patient weight of 307 kg [1]​[2].
- **Advanced Clinical Applications:** Supports a wide range of applications across radiology, cardiology, oncology, neurology, and emergency/trauma care. Enables comprehensive cardiac evaluations, enhanced lesion detectability, tumor assessment, and fast neuro assessments [1]​[2].
- **Spectral Results:** Offers a broad range of spectral imaging results, including MonoE (40–200 keV), virtual non-contrast, iodine density, Z-effective, uric acid, calcium-suppressed, and electron density maps. These results aid in tissue characterization, improved visualization of iodine-enhanced tissues, and artifact reduction [1].
- **Workflow and Automation:** The iPatient user interface and exam cards allow for streamlined, patient-centric imaging, reducing decision points and clicks, and improving scan-to-scan consistency. Spectral results can be pre-set, modified during planning, or created post-scan [1].
- **Image Quality and Reconstruction:** Utilizes advanced reconstruction algorithms such as IMR and iDose 4 for improved image quality at lower doses. The system supports high spatial and low-contrast resolution, with most reference protocols reconstructed in under one minute [1].
- **Patient Throughput:** Designed for high patient throughput, capable of handling up to 270 patients per day, with potential for more than 400 patients/day in 24-hour operation [1].
- **Energy and Environmental Considerations:** Ready-to-scan energy usage is 21 kW, with up to 149 kW during scan mode. The system is RoHS 2 and REACH compliant, with circularity programs and service/spare parts available [1].

**Clinical Benefits**
- Enables fast, confident diagnoses, potentially reducing the need for follow-up or additional scans.
- Supports advanced cardiac imaging, including coronary assessment, perfusion, and delayed enhancement studies, all without increased radiation dose.
- Provides up to 96% certainty in differentiating cysts from lesions and up to 97% diagnostic sensitivity compared to conventional CT [2].

**Technical Specifications (Selected)**
- **Generator Power:** 120 kW
- **Slices:** 512
- **Coverage:** 80 mm x 2 (double coverage)
- **Bore Size:** 800 mm
- **Rotation Speed:** 0.27s
- **Spectral MonoE Range:** 40–200 keV
- **Image Matrix:** 512 x 512 (with options for higher matrices in conventional mode)
- **Local Storage:** 7.6 TB hard drive for spectral results [1].

**Summary**
The Philips Spectral CT 7500 is a high-performance, always-on spectral CT system designed to deliver advanced diagnostic capabilities, fast workflow, and broad clinical versatility, supporting confident decision-making and efficient patient care across multiple specialties [1]​[2]​[1]​[2].

- Enables fast, confident diagnoses, potentially reducing the need for follow-up or additional scans.
- Supports advanced cardiac imaging, including coronary assessment, perfusion, and delayed enhancement studies, all without increased radiation dose.
- Provides up to 96% certainty in differentiating cysts from lesions and up to 97% diagnostic sensitivity compared to conventional CT [2].

**Technical Specifications (Selected)**
- **Generator Power:** 120 kW
- **Slices:** 512
- **Coverage:** 80 mm x 2 (double coverage)
- **Bore Size:** 800 mm
- **Rotation Speed:** 0.27s
- **Spectral MonoE Range:** 40–200 keV
- **Image Matrix:** 512 x 512 (with options for higher matrices in conventional mode)
- **Local Storage:** 7.6 TB hard drive for spectral results [1].

**Summary**
The Philips Spectral CT 7500 is a high-performance, always-on spectral CT system designed to deliver advanced diagnostic capabilities, fast workflow, and broad clinical versatility, supporting confident decision-making and efficient patient care across multiple specialties [1]​[2]​[1]​[2].
